_meta.description: Human-in-the-loop asset pipeline task board. Each item moves through stages: queued -> in-progress (human working in the external tool) -> returned (asset/link back in repo) -> tested (agents validated it) -> optimized -> implemented (live, version-bumped). Agents append/advance items; humans execute the actual tool work. See config/ai-tools.json for the tool-access matrix and reports/asset-pipeline/issues/ for full specs.

items.title: QA Knowledge-Base Build (NotebookLM)
items.description: QA designs and builds NotebookLM notebooks that become the canonical knowledge sources for the Claude AI Search to draw from almost exclusively.

items.title: Archives App (Stitch/Base44, joint QA + Architect + Designer)
items.description: Lightweight 'archive mentality' app with a thin AI-agent brain: per-directory TOC, crop-to-essential views, search/summarize over data-center-archive content.

items.title: Design Tooling Suite (Designer, free tools)
items.description: Several small, free, company-owned design/generative-AI tools, each its own optimized lightweight product with a great UI for a core user need.

items.title: Architect Org Products
items.description: Architect builds quality org-facing products using any available tool, pairing with the Designer (joint Stitch/Base44/Google AI Studio sessions) on important projects.

items.title: CommandFlow / Terminal Academy (Stitch + Base44 import)
items.description: First real imported asset from the human-in-the-loop pipeline: an interactive multi-platform terminal simulator (Bash, PowerShell, Cisco, Cloud, Networking, Security, Databases). Owner-built standalone product now lives at tools/commandflow/ (full Stitch 'Terminal Academy' design) and its shared engine/data (commandflow-core.js + commands.json) now also powers CLI Mode in the main app's AI Search tab (data-center styling). Reference pattern for future tool imports — see CLAUDE.md asset pipeline section.

history.note: Owner-built standalone product (tools/commandflow/) + CLI Mode integration committed to repo. Queued for AGENT-9 (design review), AGENT-10 (architecture review), AGENT-6 (QA testing) per reports/asset-pipeline/issues/commandflow.md.

items.title: Runbook Demo Integration (Terminal/Timeline/Metrics)
items.description: Extract Terminal Demo, Incident Timeline, and Metrics Dashboard logic from assets/incoming/datacenter-runbook-optimized/datacenter-export/dist/public/assets/ (built React/Vite components) into dependency-free vanilla-JS modules under tools/runbook/. Terminal Demo's command outputs (ping/systemctl/df) were also merged into tools/commandflow/commands.json for more realistic CLI Mode output. Remaining: wire incident-timeline.js into 'Solve a Case' mode to visualize diagnosis progress, and metrics-dashboard.js into the admin/Office tab.

history.note: tools/runbook/{terminal-demo.js, incident-timeline.js, metrics-dashboard.js} created (vanilla JS, render/mount APIs). commands.json gained the 3 demo commands (ping -c 4 192.168.1.1, systemctl status nginx, df -h /var/log). Index.html wiring into Solve-a-Case and admin/Office tab not yet done — see PENDING-WORK.md.

items.title: Database Integration Plan (D1 schema + helpers)
items.description: Plan (not yet implemented) for adopting the schema/db-helper templates from assets/incoming/database-integration-complete/database-integration-complete/templates/ (schema-template.ts, db-helpers-template.ts) for the agent simulation's D1 database. See PENDING-WORK.md for the implementation plan written this session.

history.note: Implementation plan written to PENDING-WORK.md (Feature B). Not implemented per token-budget instruction — queued for agent-10 this week.

items.title: CRM (placeholder — flagged for future serious development)
items.description: Placeholder only. The simulation's case-management needs are currently served by workers/crm-engine.js (in-memory client pool + case generator). A real CRM product is flagged for serious development at some point but is NOT scheduled and has no owner agent yet.

history.note: Seeded as a flag per Launch Decisions Part 3 — not built this session.
